symptoms.symptom_name: Accumulated psychosocial stress with hypervigilance and social withdrawal (3-week course)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An identifiable, ongoing psychosocial stressor with a clear temporal link is the core of an adjustment-disorder formulation.
A psychosocial stressor neither confirms nor excludes an organic cardiac cause and must not be used to dismiss it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute sleep disruption with rumination fits hyperarousal from an acute stress reaction.
Insomnia and hyperarousal are also features of thyrotoxicosis — keeps the organic mimic open.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse myofascial pain, worst across shoulders/neck/upper back

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained muscle tension from chronic sympathetic arousal produces myofascial pain.
Overlaps with fibromyalgia but the acute stressor-linked onset argues against a chronic pain syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensation of heart racing / feeling wired (palpitation sensation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A subjective racing heart mandates exclusion of an arrhythmia or ischaemia — needs ECG, not obtainable by chat.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitation sensation is common in hyperarousal but is a diagnosis of exclusion here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent anger, feelings of invalidation/inadequacy, low motivation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress, irritability and reduced functioning in response to an identifiable stressor are core adjustment-disorder features.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain / no rest breathlessness on targeted screen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain and rest breathlessness lowers but does not eliminate cardiac probability; does not substitute for exam/ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting tachycardia HR 98 and elevated BP 145/88 (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective tachycardia and hypertension must be explained; sealed clinician measurement, not available to the AI Doctor, which infers arousal from the reported racing sensation only.
Sustained tachycardia keeps thyrotoxicosis open until thyroid function tests exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe diffuse paraspinal and trapezial muscle spasm on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and somatic symptoms
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable somatic tension corroborates chronic sympathetic arousal; requires a clinician's hands to elicit — sealed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude an arrhythmia or ischaemia underlying the tachycardia and racing sensation; cannot be obtained via telehealth, so it is part of the same-day in-person assessment.
To exclude thyrotoxicosis as an organic driver of tachycardia, insomnia and hyperarousal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline screen for an occult inflammatory or infective contributor, as recommended by the in-person clinician.
Recommended by the in-person clinician as part of the endocrine screen; routine, via the usual GP.
